Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 193. Distributing Cloaks to Soldiers (ABL 0642)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN-ia]

(1) [To the king, my lord: your servant Nabû-pašir. Good health to the king, my lord! May Sin and Nikkal bless the king, my lord!]

o 22

[ARAD-ka mdAG—pa-šir]

o 33

[lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ia]

o 44

[d30 u dNIN.GAL]

o 55

[a-na LUGAL EN-ia lik-ru-bu]

o 66

[ina UGU-ḫi ša LUGAL EN]

(6) [As to what the king, my lord, wrote to me]: "Give [cloaks to the men of] Arbai and Šamaš-ilai!" —

o 77

[iš-pur-an-ni]

o 88

[a-na m]⸢ár-ba⸣-a.a

o 99

⸢m*d*UTU—DINGIR⸣-a.a* di-ni

o 1010

a-ki ša LUGAL EN iš-pur-an-ni

(10) just as the king, my lord, wrote to me, I have sent 200 cloaks to the At[...eans] who are in the city of [...], and given 200 cloaks to the men of Epâ who are in Til-Barsip; [all together I have g]iven out 400 cloaks.

o 1111

02* me TÚG.gul-IGI.2-[MEŠ]

o 1212

a-na KUR.at-⸢tu?⸣-[na-a.a]

b.e. 13b.e. 13

ša ina ⸢URU⸣.[x x]

ú-se-bi-[la]

(Rest destroyed or too broken for translation)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334443/.
